title: Superconducting diode effect in correlated electron systems by nonreciprocal magnetism
abstract: The superconducting diode effect (SDE), characterized by a nonreciprocal critical current in superconductors, has recently been observed in strongly correlated electron systems and near quantum criticality, pointing to unconventional mechanisms beyond weak-coupling theories. Here we investigate the SDE in the Rashba-Zeeman-Hubbard model, which captures $d$-wave superconductivity in an antiferromagnetic quantum critical regime, using the Dyson-Gor'kov equation with the fluctuation exchange approximation. We show that electron correlations suppress the conventional intrinsic SDE arising from depairing currents. More importantly, a supercurrent nonreciprocally induces antiferromagnetic order, which fundamentally governs the critical current and enables perfect diode efficiency. Our results reveal a previously unrecognized correlation-driven mechanism of the SDE and establish strongly correlated superconductors as a platform for superconducting diode physics.
summary: This paper investigates how strong electron correlations change the mechanism of the superconducting diode effect. It discovers that while correlations suppress the standard mechanism, they introduce a new effect where supercurrent induces antiferromagnetic order, allowing for highly efficient or even perfect diode behavior.
main_problem: Understanding the microscopic mechanism of the Superconducting Diode Effect (SDE) in strongly correlated electron systems and how electron correlations and quantum criticality alter it compared to weak-coupling theories.
main_result: Electron correlations suppress the conventional intrinsic SDE, but a new correlation-driven mechanism emerges where supercurrent nonrecipally induces antiferromagnetic order, potentially enabling perfect diode efficiency.
method: The authors use the Dyson-Gor'kov equations within the Fluctuation Exchange (FLEX) approximation to self-consistently calculate superconducting and magnetic properties.
model_or_system: A 2D Rashba-Zeeman-Hubbard model on a square lattice, representing d-wave superconductors near an antiferromagnetic quantum critical regime.
key_observables: Supercurrent j(p), superconducting diode efficiency (eta), critical currents (jc+ and jc-), and the Stoner factor.
important_parameters: Onsite Coulomb interaction (U), magnetic field (h), electron density (n=0.85), and temperature (T=0.005).
main_assumptions: The study uses the FLEX approximation and assumes the system is well-described by the Rashba-Zeeman-Hubbard model; it also focuses on the low-field regime (h < 0.1).
figures_summary: Figure 1(a) shows diode efficiency dependence on magnetic field for various U values; Figures 1(b)-(d) show supercurrent dependence on Cooper pair momentum.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the SDE problem, describes the Rashba-Zeeman-Hubbard model and FLEX method, presents results on the suppression of intrinsic SDE, details the emergent nonreciprocal magnetism mechanism, and concludes with the implications for correlated superconductors.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 3. Supercurrent dependence of the Stoner factor 𝛼S( 𝑗) for 𝑈= 5.5 and ℎ= 0.05, where the parameter 𝛿c is under (a) 0.008 and about (b) 0.01, (c) 0.012, (d) 0.014, (e) 0.015, and (f) 0.016. The region 𝛼S( 𝑗) > 𝛼c = 1 −𝛿c and the supercurrent that induces the AF order 𝑗AF± are colored in light and dark green, respectively. The diode efficiency is shown at the top of the figures.
